source_notes.rosstat: rosstat.gov.ru — weekly retail fuel prices by federal subject, DNR/LNR/Zaporizhzhia/Kherson rows
source_notes.telegram: Local community channels — Mariupol, Donetsk, Luhansk. Keywords: гречка, сахар, дефицит, пустые полки, очередь, тушёнка, нет топлива
source_notes.avito: avito.ru — grey market staple goods listings in occupied cities vs mainland equivalents

panic_basket.rationale: Buckwheat (гречка) is the single most reliable panic indicator in Russian/Ukrainian cultural space — disappears within hours of genuine shock. Documented in 2014 Crimea annexation, Sep 2022 mobilization announcement. Sugar and canned meat follow. Salt is extreme signal. Avito grey market activation = official retail has failed entirely.

sources: UNN, June 12 2026, citing Denys Chistikov (Deputy Permanent Representative of the President of Ukraine in Crimea).

Fuel: No strategic reserves on peninsula; arrives by tanker, consumed same-day. Sevastopol's two largest retail chains (TES, ATAN) ended free gasoline sales entirely — QR-coupon system via MAX messenger, capped at 20L/person. Euronews: 'worst fuel crisis since 2014 annexation.'

Panic goods: Confirmed shortages of гречка, rice, pasta, salt, sugar, cereals, flour via social media and local chat groups. Occupation administration recommended per-person purchase limits. Initially denied reports as 'Ukrainian propaganda' before confirming. Food stocks estimated at only a few weeks' supply; dairy self-sufficiency ~30%.

Grey market / barter: Hotel offered 10-20L fuel for 2-3 night stay; real estate agency offered seaside villa accommodation for 100L of fuel — official currency bypassed entirely for high-value transactions.
monthly_readings.fuel_score_methodology: Score 92: No reserves, tanker-only supply, two largest chains ended sales, QR-only at 20L cap. Near ceiling (100 = total cutoff). Highest-confidence fuel reading in dataset — occupation-government-acknowledged plus named Ukrainian presidential official sourcing.
monthly_readings.panic_keyword_score_methodology: Score 75: Confirmed shortages across 7 staple categories (гречка, rice, pasta, salt, sugar, cereals, flour). Official purchase limits recommended. Admin denial-then-confirmation pattern = maximum credibility signal. Weeks-only food stock estimate. Dairy at 30% self-sufficiency.
monthly_readings.avito_premium_score_methodology: Score 65: Barter economy active — villa accommodation exchanged for fuel canisters (qualitative, not scraped price premium). Represents clearest possible grey-market bypass signal; scored conservatively below scraped Avito premium because quantity/frequency unknown.
monthly_readings.note: Jun 21 AM: CCI 81→87. Crimea-specific compounding: 20L/car rationing persists, gas storage strike now threatens winter heating capacity, hotel bookings down 31%/40% Sevastopol. Sources: CBC Jun 19, MSN/Hotel Management Network Jun 12, ISW Jun 19. FIRST CRITICAL CCI READING IN DATASET. Buckwheat index active. | Jun 21 PM: CCI 87→94. Total civilian fuel sales suspension, Crimea and Sevastopol, effective 9am Jun 21 — cash, card, and coupon sales to individuals and legal entities fully halted, not just rationed. Fuel restricted to state security/essential-service agencies only (Aksyonov, Razvozhayev). Categorically worse than prior 20L/week rationing regime — complete exclusion vs limited access. Capped at 94, not full ceiling, pending confirmation of duration/permanence. Sources: NPR, Bloomberg, Euronews, RFE/RL, Meduza, Kyiv Post, all Jun 21. | Jun 24: CCI 94→100 (INDEX CEILING). Sevastopol full city blackout overnight Jun 23–24 after main substation destroyed. Public transport curfew 10pm. Large shops close 8pm. Children's summer camps suspended to Sep 1. Nighttime motorcycle ban. Black Sea Fleet families evacuating to Novorossiysk. Russian fuel crisis reaches Siberia (Omsk, Irkutsk, Novosibirsk, Kamchatka rationing). Moscow Exchange −5%, ruble past 75/dollar (weakest since May 6), Gazprom shares below 100 rubles (lowest since 2009). Multi-modal collapse: fuel + power + transport + market confidence simultaneously. Sources: Reuters Jun 23, Antikor/UNN Jun 24, Al Jazeera Jun 23, Vijesti Jun 24.

alert_thresholds.notes: Above 25 = elevated civilian stress visible in informal channels. Above 45 = multi-signal confirmation of acute shortage conditions. Above 65 = systemic civilian confidence failure — buckwheat index activating alongside fuel and grey market signals simultaneously.

divergence_note: If CCI rises while permanence ratio (Track B) holds stable, residents are riding out a temporary shock while maintaining long-term commitment — durable but stressed. If both CCI and permanence ratio move in the same direction simultaneously, behavioral change is likely imminent. Monitor divergence between 30-day survival signal and 30-year commitment signal as primary compound indicator.
